title: On-device Obsidian MCP read/write adapter + config
description: Add a config schema section declaring an on-device Obsidian MCP server (mirroring ObservabilityProvider) and a thin VaultMcpAdapter that wraps ExternalMcpServer to read, write, and list vault notes through configurable tool names, with an on-device-only guard that rejects non-local server declarations before any spawn.

successCriteria.description: The project compiles: `npm run build` (tsc) completes with no errors after the VaultSection config schema and the VaultMcpAdapter module are added.

A unit test parses a bober config containing a vault.obsidian MCP server (mcpCommand/mcpArgs/mcpEnv plus optional tool-name overrides) and asserts the parsed object round-trips with all fields preserved.

A unit test injects a fake ExternalMcpServer and asserts VaultMcpAdapter.readNote/writeNote/listNotes invoke the configured (or default cyanheads) tool names with the expected arguments and return parsed VaultNote results.

A unit test declares a server whose transport implies a non-local network endpoint and asserts the on-device guard throws/returns a clear refusal and the underlying ExternalMcpServer is never started (start not called).

nonGoals: Do not modify src/mcp/external-client.ts — wrap ExternalMcpServer, do not fork it.
Do not log or echo mcpEnv contents anywhere — treat env as opaque secrets (mirror src/mcp/external-client.ts:66-77).
Do not implement OAuth, token refresh, or any cloud Obsidian sync — on-device only.
Do not bind the adapter to a single Obsidian MCP vendor — tool names must be overridable in config.
Do not wire the adapter into the reindex CLI in this sprint — it is an independent read/write surface.
stopConditions: All success criteria sc-4-1..sc-4-4 pass under the evaluator.
`npm test` passes including the new config + adapter tests with no pre-existing test regressed.
The on-device guard rejects non-local declarations and the adapter only calls listTools/callTool on a started server for local declarations.
definitionOfDone: An on-device Obsidian MCP server can be declared in bober.config.json under a vault section (executable command + args + env + optional tool-name mapping). VaultMcpAdapter wraps the existing ExternalMcpServer stdio transport and exposes readNote/writeNote/listNotes by calling the configured tool names, parsing results into the sprint-1 VaultNote shape. Any declaration that is not on-device is refused before the server is spawned, and MCP env secrets are never logged.
assumptions: The config section mirrors ObservabilityProviderSchema (src/config/schema.ts:298-309): name + mcpCommand + mcpArgs? + mcpEnv? + enabled, added as an optional top-level `vault` section in BoberConfigSchema (src/config/schema.ts:419-450).
The adapter wraps ExternalMcpServer (src/mcp/external-client.ts:30-140) using start/listTools/callTool/stop; tests inject a fake implementing the same surface so no real subprocess spawns.
Default tool-name mapping targets cyanheads/obsidian-mcp-server (research §2 line 49); overrides allow the Obsidian Local REST API built-in MCP (research §2 line 50).

On-device determination: a stdio-spawned local executable is on-device; a declaration implying a remote/non-loopback network endpoint is rejected. The generator defines the concrete guard predicate and documents it.
outOfScope: Reconciling MCP-read notes into FactStore (that flows through the sprint-2/3 reindex path).
Choosing/installing a specific Obsidian MCP server binary for the user.
Semantic search tools (Nooscope) — only read/write/list note operations.
Connection pooling, retries, or circuit-breaking beyond what ExternalMcpServer already provides.
generatorNotes: Add VaultSectionSchema to src/config/schema.ts modeled on ObservabilityProviderSchema (lines 298-309) — an `obsidian` sub-object with name/mcpCommand/mcpArgs/mcpEnv/enabled plus an optional `toolNames` record mapping logical ops (readNote/writeNote/listNotes) to server tool names with cyanheads defaults; register it as an optional `vault` field on BoberConfigSchema (near line 449). Create src/vault/mcp-adapter.ts: VaultMcpAdapter takes an ExternalMcpServer (or a minimal interface { start, listTools, callTool, stop }) so tests inject a fake. readNote calls the configured read tool and parses the returned markdown into a VaultNote via sprint-1 parseNote; writeNote serializes a VaultNote and calls the write tool; listNotes calls the list tool. Implement an isOnDevice(provider) guard that runs BEFORE start(); on a non-local declaration throw a clear Error and never call start(). Reuse the secret-handling discipline: never include mcpEnv in errors/logs. Use the @modelcontextprotocol/sdk types already imported in external-client.ts.
evaluatorNotes: Confirm the adapter wraps (does not duplicate) ExternalMcpServer. Confirm the fake-server test asserts the exact tool NAME called and that override tool names take effect. Confirm the on-device guard test asserts start() was NEVER invoked on rejection (spy/flag on the fake). Grep the adapter for any code path that could stringify provider.mcpEnv into an error or log — there must be none. Confirm config round-trip preserves mcpEnv. Run the full suite.
